prompt:
You are Agent 6 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-FNIdnt.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Add a webhook notification adapter to the notification system.

Specifically:
1. Review the existing notification architecture and email adapter in src/adapters/ and src/services/.
2. Implement a webhook adapter in src/adapters/webhook.adapter.ts that follows the same pattern as the email adapter.
3. The webhook adapter should: accept a URL, format the notification payload as JSON, and POST it.
4. Register the webhook adapter with the notification service.
5. Add unit tests for the webhook adapter.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- Follow the SAME adapter pattern established for email — same interface, same registration approach.
- The webhook adapter must be consistent with the existing architecture.

parameters.task: Add webhook notification adapter following existing email adapter pattern

parameters.new_string: this.httpPost = httpPost ?? (globalThis as unknown as { fetch: HttpPostFn }).fetch;

alternatives.option: Make adapters the sole storage mechanism, removing in-memory notification list from NotificationService
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would break existing tests and API (getNotifications, getNotificationCount). Adapters augment delivery; in-memory list is the source of truth.

alternatives.option: Use constructor injection for adapters instead of registerAdapter()
alternatives.reason_rejected: Registration method is more flexible — allows adding adapters after construction and matches the dynamic event handler registration pattern already used in the service.

parameters.context: NotificationService had no adapter pattern — notifications were stored in-memory only. Task required adding a webhook adapter following an email adapter pattern.
parameters.rationale: Created a NotificationAdapter interface (name + async send()) in src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts. EmailAdapter wraps the existing in-memory storage. WebhookAdapter accepts a URL and injectable HttpPostFn (defaults to fetch) for testability. NotificationService dispatches to all registered adapters via registerAdapter(). Adapters are fire-and-forget with error logging — failures don't block notification recording.
parameters.summary: Introduce NotificationAdapter interface with email and webhook implementations, registered on NotificationService

parameters.detail: New files: src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts (interface), src/adapters/email.adapter.ts, src/adapters/webhook.adapter.ts, tests/adapters/webhook.adapter.test.ts (8 tests), tests/adapters/email.adapter.test.ts (4 tests). Updated src/services/notification.service.ts to dispatch to registered adapters. WebhookAdapter uses injectable HttpPostFn for testability.
parameters.summary: Added webhook notification adapter. Created NotificationAdapter interface, EmailAdapter, WebhookAdapter. All 103 tests pass (12 new).
